title: Is a 401K early withdrawal repayment penalty considered haram interest?
stanceSummary: The 401K repayment penalty is **not interest (riba)**. Interest involves a payment from one party to another third party. In this case, the penalty goes from your own pocket back into your own account — there is no third party. **Not every financial penalty is riba.** When the penalty is from you, upon you, and returned to you, there is no interest involved.
alternateQuestions: Is the penalty for taking money from my 401k haram?
Does the 401k repayment penalty count as interest (riba) in Islam?
Can Muslims take money from their 401k?
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2021 Ask Shaykh YQ video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JOEzlV6XBm4) (starting at [0:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JOEzlV6XBm4&t=0s)), Yasir Qadhi gives a concise ruling on the 401K repayment penalty.

- The 401K repayment penalty is **not riba (interest)** — it is permissible
- Riba requires a payment flowing from one party to a third party; here, the penalty goes from your pocket back into **your own account**
- Not every financial penalty constitutes interest — only when there is a third-party recipient does riba apply

---

## Full Lecture Transcript (Cleaned)

### [0:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JOEzlV6XBm4&t=0s)

Brother Tadek from New Jersey emails. He has a 401K retirement account and needs to withdraw money from it for a personal reason. However, his company requires that when he repays the withdrawal, he must put in extra money as a penalty. He asks: is this extra amount considered interest? Is it halal for him to do this?

Yasir Qadhi's response is brief and direct: **it is not interest at all**, because the money is going from your pocket back into your own pocket. There is no third party involved. **Not every penalty is interest.** When the penalty is from you, upon you, and returned to you — and no outside party receives anything — there is no riba. And that is that.
